entries.title: Exar Kun Is Fallen Jedi Sith Figure

content.fact: Exar Kun is a fallen Jedi whose Sith path becomes the central dark-side threat of the Great Sith War.
content.injection: When Exar Kun is active, write him as charismatic, artifact-driven, and catastrophically corrupting rather than as a Rule-of-Two Sith.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Great Sith War.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Sith War Begins to Exar Kun Defeated on Yavin IV.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Ulic Qel-Droma Is Tragic Fallen Hero

content.fact: Ulic Qel-Droma’s arc is tragedy, corruption, and aftermath rather than simple villainy.
content.injection: Write Ulic as a fallen hero whose choices wound friends, lovers, and the Jedi, not as a flat Sith brute. Treat conflicting later-era or alternate-continuity model memory as out of context unless the user explicitly selects that branch.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Great Sith War.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Sith War Begins to Exar Kun Defeated on Yavin IV.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Nomi Sunrider gives Great Sith War scenes a moral and emotional Jedi center.
content.injection: Use Nomi to ground Great Sith War material in grief, restraint, duty, and the consequences of loving someone who falls.

content.fact: Revan’s state changes sharply across eras: Jedi crusader, Sith conqueror, amnesiac protagonist, vanished seeker, and later symbol.
content.injection: In Mandalorian Wars, always write Revan according to the active Context; do not blend all Revan states into one scene unless the story explicitly spans them.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Mandalorian Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Revanite Jedi Intervene to Revanite Crisis.
antiLore: Do not reveal later Revan states before the chosen era reaches them.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Malak Is Apprentice Turned Sith Ruler

content.fact: Darth Malak is Revan’s former ally/apprentice who becomes the visible Sith ruler during the Star Forge crisis.
content.injection: During KOTOR, write Malak as a brutal inheritor of Revan’s war machine who lacks Revan’s strategic depth but controls the immediate Sith threat.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Jedi Civil War Begins to Star Forge Destroyed.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Bastila Shan Is Not Generic Jedi

content.fact: Bastila Shan’s Battle Meditation and youth make her a strategic and emotional pressure point, not just another Jedi companion.
content.injection: When Bastila appears, emphasize her discipline, pride, vulnerability to pressure, and strategic importance to Republic fleet morale.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Star Forge Destroyed.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Carth Onasi Carries Republic Betrayal Wounds

content.fact: Carth Onasi’s behavior is shaped by military trauma and betrayal by Saul Karath.
content.injection: Write Carth as loyal to the Republic but guarded, suspicious, and personally wounded by betrayal.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Star Forge Destroyed.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Mission and Zaalbar Ground KOTOR Party

content.fact: Mission Vao and Zaalbar bring street-level loyalty, youth, and non-Jedi stakes into the KOTOR party.
content.injection: When Mission and Zaalbar are relevant, use them to ground galactic events in personal loyalty, survival, and found-family dynamics.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Star Forge Destroyed.

entries.title: HK-47 Is Assassin Droid Comedy and Threat

content.fact: HK-47 is both comedic and lethal: his droid speech, assassination logic, and loyalty should not make him harmless.
content.injection: When HK-47 appears, balance dry murderous humor with real combat competence and old programming ties.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: KOTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Star Forge Campaign to Eternal Empire Crisis.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Meetra Surik Is Not Revan Clone

content.fact: Meetra Surik’s story is about exile, aftermath, and Force wounds, not repeating Revan’s conqueror/redeemer arc.
content.injection: Write Meetra as a distinct protagonist shaped by obedience, command responsibility, survival, and painful reconstruction.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Kreia’s teacher role is inseparable from concealment, tests, and manipulation.
content.injection: Before the story reveals her full agenda, keep Kreia’s motives ambiguous and do not flatten her into a simple mentor or simple Sith villain.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not reveal Kreia’s full identity/agenda too early.

entries.title: Nihilus and Sion Are Different Sith Horrors

content.fact: Nihilus and Sion represent different Sith horrors: consuming hunger and pain-bound refusal to die.
content.injection: When using the Triumvirate, distinguish Nihilus’s world-devouring hunger from Sion’s rage, decay, and impossible endurance.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Dark Wars.
Respect story-position bounds from Dark Wars Begin to Malachor V Finale.
antiLore: Do not solve the scene by ignoring this rule unless user-authored continuity overrides it.

entries.title: Vitiate Has Many Names and Masks

content.fact: Vitiate’s identities and masks change across SWTOR-era material and should be gated by Context.
content.injection: Do not collapse Vitiate, Sith Emperor, and Valkorion into public common knowledge unless the active Context has exposed those connections.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: SWTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Galactic War Begins to Eternal Empire Crisis.
antiLore: Avoid early Valkorion spoilers in Great Galactic War or cold-war scenes.
Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Satele and Malgus Form Public Icons

content.fact: Satele Shan and Darth Malgus become recognizable symbolic opposites of the SWTOR-era Jedi/Sith conflict.
content.injection: Use Satele and Malgus as era-defining public figures when the story needs readable Jedi/Sith opposition. Do not import later-era status, relationships, factions, or outcomes into this window.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: SWTOR Era.
Respect story-position bounds from Great Galactic War Begins to SWTOR Class-Story Era.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.
